text:
# Inventory Flow

**Inventory Flow** is an enterprise-oriented inventory management SaaS for tracking products, stock, warehouses, suppliers, purchasing, sales, and operational activity from one workspace.

> [!NOTE]
> The project is under active development. The current milestone establishes the production foundations: Clean Architecture, Identity persistence, refresh-token lifecycle rules, a responsive React shell, and a dashboard experience.

## Highlights

- **Clean .NET backend** — ASP.NET Core 9, MediatR, FluentValidation, Serilog, centralized package management, RFC 7807 errors, rate limiting, health checks, and xUnit coverage.
- **Identity-ready persistence** — SQL Server, ASP.NET Core Identity, GUID keys, refresh-token storage, EF Core migrations, and retry-aware database configuration.
- **Enterprise React UI** — React 19, TypeScript, Vite, shadcn/ui, Hugeicons, Tailwind CSS v4, responsive navigation, light/dark themes, and a code-split dashboard.
- **Professional workflow** — public repository, `main`/`develop` branches, scoped feature branches, pull requests, reviews, and automated local validation commands.

## Architecture

```text
React SPA (frontend)
        │ HTTPS / REST
ASP.NET Core API (backend/src/InventoryFlow.Api)
        │
Application → Domain ← Infrastructure
        │                  │
     MediatR            EF Core / Identity
                           │
                       SQL Server
```

```text
backend/
  src/
    InventoryFlow.Api/             HTTP pipeline and API composition
    InventoryFlow.Application/     use cases, handlers, validators, mappings
    InventoryFlow.Domain/          entities, value objects, business rules
    InventoryFlow.Infrastructure/  EF Core, Identity, persistence integrations
    InventoryFlow.Shared/          cross-cutting contracts
  tests/
    InventoryFlow.UnitTests/
    InventoryFlow.IntegrationTests/

frontend/
  src/
    app/                           configuration, providers, routing
    components/                    shadcn primitives and shared UI
    features/                      feature-owned pages, hooks, schemas, APIs
    layouts/                       route layouts
    lib/                           shared client infrastructure
    store/                         Zustand client-only state
```

## Technology

| Area | Tools |
| --- | --- |
| Backend | ASP.NET Core 9, C#, EF Core, SQL Server, ASP.NET Identity, MediatR, FluentValidation, AutoMapper, Serilog |
| Frontend | React 19, TypeScript, Vite, React Router v7, TanStack Query/Table, React Hook Form, Zod, Axios, Zustand |
| Design | shadcn/ui, Tailwind CSS v4, Hugeicons, Recharts, Sonner, Motion |
| Tooling | Bun, xUnit, EF Core local tool manifest |

## Get started

### Prerequisites

- [.NET SDK 9.0.316](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download/dotnet/9.0), pinned in `global.json`
- [Bun 1.3+](https://bun.sh/)
- SQL Server 2022+ (local, containerized, or hosted)

### 1. Configure the database

Set the connection string in your shell before running migrations or database-backed features:

```bash
export ConnectionStrings__InventoryFlowDatabase='Server=localhost,1433;Database=InventoryFlow;User Id=sa;Password=<strong-password>;TrustServerCertificate=True'
```

> [!IMPORTANT]
> Do not commit real credentials. Environment files and local agent state are ignored by Git.

### 2. Install dependencies and apply migrations

```bash
cd backend
dotnet tool restore
dotnet restore
dotnet ef database update --project src/InventoryFlow.Infrastructure --startup-project src/InventoryFlow.Api
cd ../frontend
bun install
cp .env.example .env
```

### 3. Run the application

Use two terminals from the repository root:

```bash
# API
cd backend
dotnet run --project src/InventoryFlow.Api
```

```bash
# Web client
cd frontend
bun run dev
```

The API health endpoint is available at `http://localhost:5255/health` when using the default launch profile. The frontend runs on `http://localhost:5173`.

## Quality checks

```bash
# Backend
dotnet build backend/InventoryFlow.sln
dotnet test backend/InventoryFlow.sln
dotnet format backend/InventoryFlow.sln --verify-no-changes --no-restore

# Frontend
cd frontend
bun run typecheck
bun run lint
bun run build
bunx prettier --check .
```

## Delivery workflow

- `main` contains production-ready releases.
- `develop` is the integration branch.
- Frontend work uses `feature/frontend/<feature-name>`.
- Backend work uses `feature/backend/<feature-name>`.
- Fixes use `bugfix/frontend/<description>` or `bugfix/backend/<description>`.

Every change is committed on a scoped branch, validated locally, reviewed in a pull request into `develop`, and promoted to `main` for release.

## Current scope

Implemented foundations include the API pipeline, global problem-details handling, health checks, SQL Server Identity schema, refresh-token domain model and migration, and the responsive dashboard shell.

Upcoming vertical slices cover authentication (JWT and token rotation), role and permission enforcement, catalog management, inventory movements, purchase and sales orders, reporting, Redis caching, Hangfire jobs, Docker Compose, and CI/CD.

- **Identity-ready persistence** — SQL Server, ASP.NET Core Identity, GUID keys, refresh-token storage, EF Core migrations, and retry-aware database configuration.
- **Enterprise React UI** — React 19, TypeScript, Vite, shadcn/ui, Hugeicons, Tailwind CSS v4, responsive navigation, light/dark themes, and a code-split dashboard.
- **Professional workflow** — public repository, `main`/`develop` branches, scoped feature branches, pull requests, reviews, and automated local validation commands.

## Architecture

```text
React SPA (frontend)
        │ HTTPS / REST
ASP.NET Core API (backend/src/InventoryFlow.Api)
        │
Application → Domain ← Infrastructure
        │                  │
     MediatR            EF Core / Identity
                           │
                       SQL Server
```

```text
backend/
  src/
    InventoryFlow.Api/             HTTP pipeline and API composition
    InventoryFlow.Application/     use cases, handlers, validators, mappings
    InventoryFlow.Domain/          entities, value objects, business rules
    InventoryFlow.Infrastructure/  EF Core, Identity, persistence integrations
    InventoryFlow.Shared/          cross-cutting contracts
  tests/
    InventoryFlow.UnitTests/
    InventoryFlow.IntegrationTests/

frontend/
  src/
    app/                           configuration, providers, routing
    components/                    shadcn primitives and shared UI
    features/                      feature-owned pages, hooks, schemas, APIs
    layouts/                       route layouts
    lib/                           shared client infrastructure
    store/                         Zustand client-only state
```

## Technology

| Area | Tools |
| --- | --- |
| Backend | ASP.NET Core 9, C#, EF Core, SQL Server, ASP.NET Identity, MediatR, FluentValidation, AutoMapper, Serilog |
| Frontend | React 19, TypeScript, Vite, React Router v7, TanStack Query/Table, React Hook Form, Zod, Axios, Zustand |
| Design | shadcn/ui, Tailwind CSS v4, Hugeicons, Recharts, Sonner, Motion |
| Tooling | Bun, xUnit, EF Core local tool manifest |

## Get started

### Prerequisites

- [.NET SDK 9.0.316](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download/dotnet/9.0), pinned in `global.json`
- [Bun 1.3+](https://bun.sh/)
- SQL Server 2022+ (local, containerized, or hosted)

### 1. Configure the database

Set the connection string in your shell before running migrations or database-backed features:

```bash
export ConnectionStrings__InventoryFlowDatabase='Server=localhost,1433;Database=InventoryFlow;User Id=sa;Password=<strong-password>;TrustServerCertificate=True'
```

> [!IMPORTANT]
> Do not commit real credentials. Environment files and local agent state are ignored by Git.

### 2. Install dependencies and apply migrations

```bash
cd backend
dotnet tool restore
dotnet restore
dotnet ef database update --project src/InventoryFlow.Infrastructure --startup-project src/InventoryFlow.Api
cd ../frontend
bun install
cp .env.example .env
```

### 3. Run the application

Use two terminals from the repository root:

```bash
# API
cd backend
dotnet run --project src/InventoryFlow.Api
```

```bash
# Web client
cd frontend
bun run dev
```

The API health endpoint is available at `http://localhost:5255/health` when using the default launch profile. The frontend runs on `http://localhost:5173`.

## Quality checks

```bash
# Backend
dotnet build backend/InventoryFlow.sln
dotnet test backend/InventoryFlow.sln
dotnet format backend/InventoryFlow.sln --verify-no-changes --no-restore

# Frontend
cd frontend
bun run typecheck
bun run lint
bun run build
bunx prettier --check .
```

## Delivery workflow

- `main` contains production-ready releases.
- `develop` is the integration branch.
- Frontend work uses `feature/frontend/<feature-name>`.
- Backend work uses `feature/backend/<feature-name>`.
- Fixes use `bugfix/frontend/<description>` or `bugfix/backend/<description>`.

Every change is committed on a scoped branch, validated locally, reviewed in a pull request into `develop`, and promoted to `main` for release.

## Current scope

Implemented foundations include the API pipeline, global problem-details handling, health checks, SQL Server Identity schema, refresh-token domain model and migration, and the responsive dashboard shell.

Upcoming vertical slices cover authentication (JWT and token rotation), role and permission enforcement, catalog management, inventory movements, purchase and sales orders, reporting, Redis caching, Hangfire jobs, Docker Compose, and CI/CD.

# Inventory Flow Web

The React single-page application for Inventory Flow.

## Stack

React 19, TypeScript, Vite, shadcn/ui, Tailwind CSS v4, React Router v7, TanStack Query/Table, React Hook Form, Zod, Axios, Zustand, Hugeicons, Recharts, Sonner, and Motion.

## Commands

```bash
bun install
bun run dev
bun run typecheck
bun run lint
bun run build
```

## Configuration

Copy `.env.example` to `.env` and set `VITE_API_BASE_URL` to the API origin. The default is `http://localhost:5000`.

## Structure

- `src/app`: application configuration, providers, and routing
- `src/components`: generated shadcn/ui primitives and shared layout components
- `src/features`: feature-owned UI, API clients, schemas, types, and hooks
- `src/layouts`: route layouts
- `src/lib`: shared client infrastructure
- `src/store`: Zustand client-only state

==== README.md 1 # Inventory Flow 2 3 **Inventory Flow** is an enterprise-oriented inventory management SaaS for tracking products, stock, warehouses, suppliers, purchasing, sales, and operational activity from one workspace. 4 5 > [!NOTE] 6 > The project is under active development. The current milestone establishes the production foundations: Clean Architecture, Identity persistence, refresh-token lifecycle rules, a responsive React shell, and a dashboard experience. 7 8 ## Highlights 9 10 - **Clean .NET backend** — ASP.NET Core 9, MediatR, FluentValidation, Serilog, centralized package management, RFC 7807 errors, rate limiting, health checks, and xUnit coverage. 11 - **Identity-ready persistence** — SQL Server, ASP.NET Core Identity, GUID keys, refresh-token storage, EF Core migrations, and retry-aware database configuration. 12 - **Enterprise React UI** — React 19, TypeScript, Vite, shadcn/ui, Hugeicons, Tailwind CSS v4, responsive navigation, light/dark themes, and a code-split dashboard. 13 - **Professional workflow** — public repository, `main`/`develop` branches, scoped feature branches, pull requests, reviews, and automated local validation commands. 14 15 ## Architecture 16 17 ```text 18 React SPA (frontend) 19 │ HTTPS / REST 20 ASP.NET Core API (backend/src/InventoryFlow.Api) 21 │ 22 Application → Domain ← Infrastructure 23 │ │ 24 MediatR EF Core / Identity 25 │ 26 SQL Server 27 ``` 28 29 ```text 30 backend/ 31 src/ 32 InventoryFlow.Api/ HTTP pipeline and API composition 33 InventoryFlow.Application/ use cases, handlers, validators, mappings 34 InventoryFlow.Domain/ entities, value objects, business rules 35 InventoryFlow.Infrastructure/ EF Core, Identity, persistence integrations 36 InventoryFlow.Shared/ cross-cutting contracts 37 tests/ 38 InventoryFlow.UnitTests/ 39 InventoryFlow.IntegrationTests/ 40 41 frontend/ 42 src/ 43 app/ configuration, providers, routing 44 components/ shadcn primitives and shared UI 45 features/ feature-owned pages, hooks, schemas, APIs 46 layouts/ route layouts 47 lib/ shared client infrastructure 48 store/ Zustand client-only state 49 ``` 50 51 ## Technology 52 53 | Area | Tools | 54 | --- | --- | 55 | Backend | ASP.NET Core 9, C#, EF Core, SQL Server, ASP.NET Identity, MediatR, FluentValidation, AutoMapper, Serilog | 56 | Frontend | React 19, TypeScript, Vite, React Router v7, TanStack Query/Table, React Hook Form, Zod, Axios, Zustand | 57 | Design | shadcn/ui, Tailwind CSS v4, Hugeicons, Recharts, Sonner, Motion | 58 | Tooling | Bun, xUnit, EF Core local tool manifest | 59 60 ## Get started 61 62 ### Prerequisites 63 64 - [.NET SDK 9.0.316](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download/dotnet/9.0), pinned in `global.json` 65 - [Bun 1.3+](https://bun.sh/) 66 - SQL Server 2022+ (local, containerized, or hosted) 67 68 ### 1. Configure the database 69 70 Set the connection string in your shell before running migrations or database-backed features: 71 72 ```bash 73 export ConnectionStrings__InventoryFlowDatabase='Server=localhost,1433;Database=InventoryFlow;User Id=sa;Password=;TrustServerCertificate=True' 74 ``` 75 76 > [!IMPORTANT] 77 > Do not commit real credentials. Environment files and local agent state are ignored by Git. 78 79 ### 2. Install dependencies and apply migrations 80 81 ```bash 82 cd backend 83 dotnet tool restore 84 dotnet restore 85 dotnet ef database update --project src/InventoryFlow.Infrastructure --startup-project src/InventoryFlow.Api 86 cd ../frontend 87 bun install 88 cp .env.example .env 89 ``` 90 91 ### 3. Run the application 92 93 Use two terminals from the repository root: 94 95 ```bash 96 # API 97 cd backend 98 dotnet run --project src/InventoryFlow.Api 99 ``` 100 101 ```bash 102 # Web client 103 cd frontend 104 bun run dev 105 ``` 106 107 The API health endpoint is available at `http://localhost:5255/health` when using the default launch profile. The frontend runs on `http://localhost:5173`. 108 109 ## Quality checks 110 111 ```bash 112 # Backend 113 dotnet build backend/InventoryFlow.sln 114 dotnet test backend/InventoryFlow.sln 115 dotnet format backend/InventoryFlow.sln --verify-no-changes --no-restore 116 117 # Frontend 118 cd frontend 119 bun run typecheck 120 bun run lint 121 bun run build 122 bunx prettier --check . 123 ``` 124 125 ## Delivery workflow 126 127 - `main` contains production-ready releases. 128 - `develop` is the integration branch. 129 - Frontend work uses `feature/frontend/`. 130 - Backend work uses `feature/backend/`. 131 - Fixes use `bugfix/frontend/` or `bugfix/backend/`. 132 133 Every change is committed on a scoped branch, validated locally, reviewed in a pull request into `develop`, and promoted to `main` for release. 134 135 ## Current scope 136 137 Implemented foundations include the API pipeline, global problem-details handling, health checks, SQL Server Identity schema, refresh-token domain model and migration, and the responsive dashboard shell. 138 139 Upcoming vertical slices cover authentication (JWT and token rotation), role and permission enforcement, catalog management, inventory movements, purchase and sales orders, reporting, Redis caching, Hangfire jobs, Docker Compose, and CI/CD. ==== backend/src/InventoryFlow.Api/Program.cs 1 using System.Threading.RateLimiting; # Code Context

## Files Retrieved
1. `README.md` (lines 3-13, 15-49, 109-139) - product definition, Clean Architecture boundaries, quality commands, and explicitly ordered upcoming slices.
2. `backend/src/InventoryFlow.Api/Program.cs` (lines 8-59) - API composition has controllers, authorization middleware, CORS, rate limiting, and error handling, but no authentication registration/middleware.
3. `backend/src/InventoryFlow.Infrastructure/DependencyInjection/InfrastructureServiceCollectionExtensions.cs` (lines 23-59) - SQL Server, Identity, roles, SignInManager, password/lockout policy, and persistent data protection are already registered.
4. `backend/src/InventoryFlow.Domain/Entities/RefreshToken.cs` (lines 6-92) - a persisted, hashed, UTC-validated and revocable refresh-token aggregate is ready for token rotation.
5. `backend/src/InventoryFlow.Infrastructure/Persistence/ApplicationDbContext.cs` (lines 8-29) and `backend/src/InventoryFlow.Infrastructure/Persistence/Configurations/RefreshTokenConfiguration.cs` (lines 11-35) - Identity, refresh tokens, uniqueness and user foreign key are already in the schema.
6. `frontend/src/app/router/index.tsx` (lines 1-46) - all application routes are public; Products and all operational pages are placeholders.
7. `frontend/src/lib/api-client.ts` (lines 1-10) and `frontend/src/app/providers/AppProviders.tsx` (lines 1-22) - Axios and TanStack Query/Toaster foundations exist, but no bearer injection, auth state, or route guard exists.
8. `frontend/src/features/dashboard/pages/DashboardPage.tsx` (lines 40-96, 98-260) - dashboard content is hard-coded demo data, not a backend integration target yet.
9. `backend/tests/InventoryFlow.IntegrationTests/Api/InventoryFlowApiFactory.cs` (lines 10-58) and `backend/tests/InventoryFlow.IntegrationTests/Api/HealthEndpointTests.cs` (lines 9-34) - existing API integration test hosting pattern; only health is covered.

## Key Code

### Prioritized recommendation
**P0 — Deliver authenticated workspace access: register, login, refresh-token rotation, logout, and protected SPA shell.**

This is the next smallest viable vertical slice. It turns the existing Identity and refresh-token persistence foundations into a user-facing, secure workspace boundary, makes the dashboard an authenticated destination rather than publicly exposed mock UI, and unblocks the next P1 catalog slice without retrofitting authorization/session handling. It is explicitly the first upcoming slice in `README.md:139` and requires no new inventory schema.

**P1 immediately after P0 — Product catalog CRUD (list/create/edit/archive) for the signed-in user/workspace.** This is the first direct inventory-record value, but should not precede P0 because the current app has neither authenticated principal nor tenant/workspace model. Avoid warehouses, stock movements, purchasing, sales, and reporting until catalog identity/ownership semantics are settled.

### P0 scope and acceptance criteria
1. `POST /api/auth/register` validates display name, email, and the existing Identity password policy; creates the Identity user and returns a short-lived JWT plus refresh token according to one documented transport choice.
2. `POST /api/auth/login` accepts valid credentials, honors Identity lockout failures, and returns the same session contract. Invalid credentials disclose no account-existence detail.
3. `POST /api/auth/refresh` accepts an active refresh token, atomically revokes it, persists a replacement hashed token, and returns a new access/refresh pair. Reusing a revoked, expired, malformed, or unknown refresh token returns 401 and creates no session.
4. `POST /api/auth/logout` revokes the supplied active refresh token; calling refresh afterward is unauthorized. Refresh tokens are never persisted or logged in plaintext.
5. JWT bearer validation (issuer, audience, signing key, expiry) is configured from non-committed configuration; `UseAuthentication()` precedes `UseAuthorization()`. A small protected `GET /api/auth/me` proves the principal/claims pipeline.
6. SPA supplies Login and Register pages; unauthenticated visitors to `/dashboard` and other workspace routes redirect to login; valid login returns to the requested route. The Axios client attaches the access token and performs one controlled refresh/retry or redirects to login on session expiry.
7. Unit tests cover refresh-token issuance/rotation edge cases; integration tests cover registration, login, protected endpoint 401/200, rotation, reuse rejection, and logout. Frontend typecheck/lint/build remain clean.

### Likely affected modules
- **Backend API:** new `Controllers/AuthController.cs` (or route group), `Program.cs`, `appsettings*.json`, API project package references for JWT bearer.
- **Application:** `Features/Authentication` commands/queries, DTOs, validators, handlers; abstractions for clock/token issuer/refresh-token repository as needed. This preserves the documented API → Application → Domain ← Infrastructure flow (`README.md:17-26`).
- **Infrastructure:** JWT configuration/token service, Identity-facing authentication implementation, `ApplicationDbContext` usage/repository; reuse `RefreshToken` and its mapping rather than adding a second token table.
- **Tests:** extend `InventoryFlowApiFactory.cs` only as necessary for deterministic JWT/configuration; add authentication integration tests and application/domain unit tests.
- **Frontend:** `features/auth/` (pages, form schemas, API client/hooks/types), auth/session store/provider, router guard and login/register routes in `app/router/index.tsx`, Axios interceptors in `lib/api-client.ts`; optionally update Topbar to display the authenticated user/logout.

### Dependencies / decisions to make in implementation
- Add `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authentication.JwtBearer`; it is absent from central package versions and API project references.
- Provide `Jwt` settings through environment/secret configuration (signing key must not be committed). CORS needs a real frontend origin: current `Cors:AllowedOrigins` is empty (`appsettings.json:2-4`).
- Choose refresh transport deliberately: secure HttpOnly/SameSite cookie is preferred for browser use; if JSON body/local storage is selected, document the XSS tradeoff and do not persist tokens in Zustand/localStorage by default.
- Define initial registration policy (open self-service versus seeded/invite-only admin). Do **not** add roles/permissions to P0 beyond claims plumbing; roadmap places enforcement after authentication.
- A workspace/tenant ownership model is not present. P1 must first decide whether Product ownership is user- or organization-based; do not bake user-only ownership into a future SaaS multi-tenant schema without a product decision.

## Architecture

`Program.cs` composes Application and Infrastructure, while Infrastructure currently provides Identity's `UserManager`, `SignInManager`, EF store, and role services. The missing link is an Application-owned authentication use case that invokes those services through abstractions and delegates JWT creation/persistence to Infrastructure. The API exposes that contract; the React auth feature calls it through the existing Axios client, owns transient session state, and gates the existing `DashboardLayout` routes. The existing `RefreshToken` object supports the critical rotate/revoke behavior and database indexes support lookup by hash and user/expiry.

## Review Findings

- **High — `backend/src/InventoryFlow.Api/Program.cs:15-18,44-49`:** authorization is enabled without authentication service or `UseAuthentication`; no endpoint can establish a principal or enforce bearer credentials. P0 resolves this.
- **High — `frontend/src/app/router/index.tsx:23-45`:** the complete workspace shell is publicly routable. This conflicts with the Identity-ready backend and makes future inventory data exposure unsafe.
- **Medium — `frontend/src/features/dashboard/pages/DashboardPage.tsx:40-96`:** all inventory/sales/activity numbers are static. They must not be interpreted as inventory data or used as an API contract; defer dashboard integration until catalog/movement data exists.
- **Medium — `backend/src/InventoryFlow.Api/appsettings.json:2-4`:** CORS permits no configured SPA origin, so a browser auth flow will fail cross-origin until deployment/environment config supplies it.
- **Low — current branch/diff:** HEAD is `feature/backend/persistence-and-identity-foundation` at `2bec0f1`; the three latest commits establish Identity persistence/data-protection keys. `git diff` has no tracked source changes; only ignored/untracked `.pi-subagents/` artifacts are present.

## Start Here

Open `backend/src/InventoryFlow.Api/Program.cs` first. It is the narrow composition point where the missing JWT authentication services and middleware must be added before any controller, token service, or SPA guard can become an end-to-end session.
